timeline.what: **Confirm your cause first** (see above), then start the *behaviour + food* levers immediately — cut high-glycemic carbs and trial removing milk/whey. If using a topical retinoid, start every third night to build tolerance.
Expect an initial **'purge'** on a retinoid — things can look worse before better as clogged pores clear. Skin may be dry and flaky; do not stop. Dietary changes show little yet.
The dairy and glycemic-load changes start to show — fewer new inflamed spots. Retinoid tolerance is up; move toward nightly use.
The honest checkpoint. A real regimen shows a **clear drop in new breakouts** by now. Post-inflammatory marks fade slowly over months after the active acne settles.

acne.working: Fewer *new* inflamed spots week over week (count them — memory lies), less oiliness by mid-afternoon, and existing spots resolving without leaving deep marks. You are chasing the trend, not a clear face overnight.
acne.reassess: See a doctor early — don't self-treat for months — if you have **painful, deep nodules or cysts**, scarring, acne that suddenly worsens or covers the back/chest, or breakouts with irregular periods and excess facial/body hair (possible PCOS). These have prescription routes that prevent permanent scarring topicals can't.

context.who: Women with irregular cycles / jawline acne
context.mod: This is often the hormonal (androgen) route — a doctor-led hormonal option frequently outperforms topicals alone. Worth raising early.
Rule out a trigger first: a new medication, cosmetic, or high stress. Skin is drier, so retinoids need slower titration and more moisturiser.
Shower and change out of occlusive kit promptly (mechanical/'acne mechanica'); whey protein is a common hidden dairy-and-insulin driver worth trialling off.

troubleshooting.issue: No change after 12 weeks of a consistent regimen
troubleshooting.fix: You're likely treating the wrong cause. Re-check which cause fits you above — a diet lever won't fix a comedogenic-product or hormonal driver, and vice-versa. This is the point to see a clinician.

troubleshooting.issue: Retinoid made it much worse / too irritating
troubleshooting.fix: That's usually the expected purge + over-use. Drop frequency, apply a pea-sized amount to dry skin, buffer with moisturiser, and stop other actives. If it's genuine burning or swelling, stop and ask a pharmacist/doctor.

troubleshooting.issue: Only the jaw/chin breaks out, cyclically
troubleshooting.fix: Points to the hormonal route rather than diet or products — prioritise the hormonal cause and a doctor's assessment over stacking more topicals.
